methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $39.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.5B - $14.0B.

data.narrative:
Figure AI, a Series C AI & Software company founded by Brett Adcock, has raised $1.8B+ and carries a current post-money valuation of $39.0B. The peer universe comprises 9 comparable companies (1 public, 8 private) in the same sector and stage, including Skild AI ($14.0B), Neuralink ($9.7B), Luma AI ($4.0B), ElevenLabs ($3.0B), and Runway ($1.5B).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $39.0B base, implying $66.3B. Peer-median analysis across the 6-company same-stage, same-sector sample yields a median valuation of $3.5B, with a range spanning $1.5B to $14.0B. The substantial dispersion reflects heterogeneity in business maturity, revenue, and capital intensity within the frontier AI cohort.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $3.0B to $76.2B, with a median of $34.9B. The current $39.0B valuation sits modestly above the calculated median, within the plausible high-end of the range. Key uncertainties include product commercialization trajectory, unit economics at scale, and whether stage-progression multipliers derived from earlier software cohorts remain valid in the current frontier AI environment. The peer-median method's wide dispersion underscores limited comparability within this nascent sector.
